vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of advanced degradation. The expected specimens (P1-P4) are not visually identifiable as living botanical entities. The 'White Rabbit' anchor is present in the lower pot, but the primary occupants are absent or reduced to necrotic debris.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss (Yellow pot contains only soil/debris)
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss (Black pot contains only soil/debris)
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss (Black pot contains only soil/debris and White Rabbit anchor)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (Shared pot with P2 is void of life)

plant_audit.P1: No visible foliage. Soil surface appears undisturbed but void of the String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: No visible foliage. Mexican Mint is absent.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos is absent. Only the White Rabbit figurine remains in the soil.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; no signs of active root growth or surface sprouts.
biome_observations.fungal_presence: None detected, though the lack of plant matter suggests a dormant or sterile substrate.
biome_observations.debris: White ceramic/plastic fragments (likely from the rabbit or pot degradation) are scattered in the P3 pot.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a static, non-living environment. No growth, movement, or recovery occurred over the 5-day observation window. The environment is effectively a 'dead' biome.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: Total systemic failure. The absence of chlorophyll-bearing tissue and the presence of only inert soil and static debris indicate that the plants have either been removed or have completely decomposed/withered prior to the start of this sequence.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a spoon in the earliest image and a white cylindrical object (likely a sensor or marker) in the later images suggests human intervention, but no botanical recovery.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms a complete absence of living botanical specimens. The pots serve as containers for inert soil and a single decorative figurine. There is no evidence of recent plant life, suggesting the biome has been inactive for an extended period.
